name: Cain Kills Abel
description: The first murder in human history. Cain, jealous that God accepted Abel's offering but rejected his, rose up and killed his brother in the field.

name: War of the Nine Kings
description: Four Mesopotamian kings warred against five kings of the Jordan plain in the Valley of Siddim. Lot was captured. Abram pursued with 318 trained men and rescued Lot near Dan.
belligerents: Five Kings of the Jordan Plain
result: Eastern kings victorious initially; Abram rescued Lot

name: Abram's Rescue of Lot
description: Abram armed 318 trained servants, pursued the four kings to Dan, divided his forces by night, and defeated them, recovering Lot, the women, and all the goods.

name: Destruction of Sodom and Gomorrah
description: God rained fire and brimstone from heaven upon Sodom, Gomorrah, and the cities of the plain for their wickedness. Only Lot and his daughters escaped.

name: The Ten Plagues of Egypt
description: God struck Egypt with ten plagues — blood, frogs, gnats, flies, livestock death, boils, hail, locusts, darkness, and death of the firstborn — to free Israel from bondage.

name: Crossing of the Red Sea
description: God parted the Red Sea for Israel to cross, then collapsed the waters on Pharaoh's pursuing army, destroying it entirely.

name: Battle of Rephidim
description: Israel fought Amalek. As long as Moses held up his hands, Israel prevailed. Aaron and Hur held up his arms until sunset.

name: Defeat at Hormah
description: After refusing to enter Canaan at Kadesh, Israelites presumed to go up anyway against God's command. The Amalekites and Canaanites struck them down to Hormah.

name: Victory at Hormah
description: The Canaanite king of Arad attacked Israel. Israel vowed to the LORD and He delivered the Canaanites. They utterly destroyed their cities.

name: Defeat of Sihon King of Heshbon
description: Israel requested passage through Sihon's territory. Sihon refused and attacked. Israel defeated him and took all his cities including Heshbon.

name: Defeat of Og King of Bashan
description: Og came out to battle against Israel at Edrei. God told Moses not to fear him. Israel struck Og, his sons, and all his people until none remained.

name: War Against Midian
description: God commanded Moses to avenge Israel against Midian for the Baal-Peor incident. Twelve thousand soldiers killed the five kings of Midian and Balaam son of Beor.

name: Battle of Jericho
description: Joshua led the Israelites around the walls of Jericho for seven days. On the seventh day, the walls fell and the city was taken.

name: First Battle of Ai (Defeat)
description: Israel attacked Ai overconfidently. Because of Achan's sin in taking devoted things at Jericho, God withdrew His support and Israel fled before Ai's men.

name: Second Battle of Ai (Victory)
description: After Achan's sin was dealt with, Israel conquered Ai through an ambush strategy commanded by God. Joshua set an ambush behind the city and drew out its defenders.

name: Battle of Gibeon
description: Joshua defeated five Amorite kings. God cast down great hailstones and the sun stood still over Gibeon.

name: Conquest of Makkedah
description: After Gibeon, Joshua pursued the fleeing Amorites. The five kings hid in a cave at Makkedah. Joshua sealed, then executed them, and took the city.

name: Conquest of Libnah
description: Joshua moved from Makkedah to Libnah and captured it, striking every person in it with the edge of the sword.

name: Conquest of Lachish
description: Joshua besieged and took Lachish on the second day. The king of Gezer came to help Lachish but was also defeated.

name: Conquest of Eglon
description: Joshua went up from Lachish to Eglon and captured it that same day, devoting it to destruction.

name: Conquest of Hebron
description: Joshua went up from Eglon to Hebron and captured it, striking it and all its towns with the edge of the sword.

name: Conquest of Debir
description: Joshua turned back to Debir and captured it along with its king and all its towns.

name: Battle of the Waters of Merom
description: Joshua defeated a coalition of northern Canaanite kings led by Jabin of Hazor at the waters of Merom.

name: Burning of Hazor
description: After the victory at Merom, Joshua turned back and captured Hazor, the head of all those kingdoms, and burned it with fire.

name: Othniel vs Cushan-rishathaim
description: After eight years of oppression by Cushan-rishathaim of Mesopotamia, the Spirit of the LORD came upon Othniel. He judged Israel and defeated Cushan-rishathaim.

name: Ehud's Assassination and Moab's Defeat
description: The left-handed Ehud assassinated Eglon king of Moab with a concealed sword, then rallied Israel. They seized the fords of the Jordan and killed about 10,000 Moabites.

name: Shamgar vs Philistines
description: Shamgar son of Anath struck down 600 Philistines with an ox goad, saving Israel.

name: Battle of Mount Tabor
description: Deborah and Barak defeated Sisera's 900 iron chariots at the Kishon River. Jael killed Sisera with a tent peg.

name: Gideon's Victory over Midian
description: With only 300 men armed with trumpets and torches, Gideon routed the vast Midianite army by God's design.

name: Abimelech's Destruction of Shechem
description: Abimelech, Gideon's son by a concubine, killed 70 brothers to seize power. When Shechem revolted, he razed the city and sowed it with salt, killing over 1,000 in the tower of Shechem.
result: Abimelech destroyed Shechem; killed at Thebez by a millstone

name: Jephthah vs Ammonites
description: The Spirit of the LORD came upon Jephthah and he defeated the Ammonites, striking twenty cities from Aroer to Minnith with a very great slaughter.

name: Jephthah vs Ephraim (Shibboleth)
description: Ephraim accused Jephthah of not calling them to fight. Civil war erupted. Gileadites seized the Jordan fords and used 'Shibboleth' as a test, killing 42,000 Ephraimites.

name: Samson's Raid on Ashkelon
description: Angered over a riddle bet, the Spirit of the LORD came upon Samson and he struck down thirty men of Ashkelon to pay his wager.

name: Samson at Lehi (Jawbone Hill)
description: Bound and delivered to the Philistines, Samson broke his ropes and struck down 1,000 men with the jawbone of a donkey.

name: Samson Destroys the Temple of Dagon
description: Blinded and enslaved, Samson was brought to the temple of Dagon to be mocked. He pushed apart the pillars, killing himself and about 3,000 Philistines.
result: Mutual destruction; Samson died with more kills in death than in life

name: Israel vs Benjamin at Gibeah (Day 1-2)
description: After the atrocity at Gibeah, Israel assembled against Benjamin. On the first two days, Benjamin killed 40,000 Israelites despite being outnumbered.

name: Israel vs Benjamin at Gibeah (Day 3)
description: On the third day, Israel used an ambush strategy. God struck Benjamin before Israel. 25,100 Benjaminites fell. Only 600 men survived, fleeing to the rock of Rimmon.

name: First Battle of Ebenezer (Ark Captured)
description: Israel was defeated and the Ark of the Covenant was captured by the Philistines. Eli's sons Hophni and Phinehas died.

name: Battle of Mizpah
description: Samuel called Israel to repentance at Mizpah. The Philistines attacked, but the LORD thundered with a great thunder against them and they were routed.
result: Israelite victory; Samuel raised Ebenezer stone

name: Relief of Jabesh-gilead
description: Nahash the Ammonite besieged Jabesh-gilead, demanding to gouge out every right eye. Saul rallied Israel, divided his army into three companies, and routed the Ammonites by morning.
result: Israelite victory; Saul confirmed as king

name: Battle of Michmash
description: Jonathan and his armor-bearer climbed a cliff and struck down the Philistine garrison, sparking a rout.

name: Saul's War Against the Amalekites
description: God commanded Saul to utterly destroy Amalek. Saul defeated them but spared King Agag and the best livestock, leading to his rejection as king.
result: Israelite military victory; Saul rejected by God

name: Valley of Elah (David and Goliath)
description: The shepherd boy David killed the Philistine giant Goliath with a single stone from his sling, turning the battle.

name: Battle of Mount Gilboa
description: King Saul and his son Jonathan were killed in battle against the Philistines on Mount Gilboa.

name: Battle at the Pool of Gibeon
description: Abner (for Ish-bosheth) and Joab (for David) met at the pool of Gibeon. Twelve men from each side fought to the death, then a fierce battle erupted. David's men won.

name: David Captures Jerusalem
description: David and his men took the Jebusite stronghold of Zion, the City of David, making it his capital. Joab went up first through the water shaft.

name: Battle of Baal-perazim
description: The Philistines spread out in the Valley of Rephaim. David inquired of the LORD, attacked, and broke through them like a flood.

name: Second Battle of Rephaim (Mulberry Trees)
description: Philistines returned to the Valley of Rephaim. God told David to circle behind and wait for the sound of marching in the mulberry trees, then strike. David obeyed and routed them.

name: War Against Ammon and Aram
description: Ammon hired Aramean mercenaries against Israel. Joab divided his forces — he fought the Arameans while Abishai fought the Ammonites. Both enemies fled.

name: Siege of Rabbah
description: Joab besieged the Ammonite capital of Rabbah. During this siege, David arranged for Uriah's death.

name: Battle of the Forest of Ephraim
description: Absalom rebelled against David. The decisive battle was fought in the forest of Ephraim, where 20,000 fell. Absalom's hair caught in an oak tree and Joab killed him.

name: Sheba's Rebellion at Abel Beth-maacah
description: Sheba son of Bichri led a revolt against David after Absalom's defeat. Joab pursued him to Abel Beth-maacah. A wise woman had Sheba beheaded and threw his head over the wall.

name: Shishak's Invasion of Judah
description: Pharaoh Shishak invaded Judah with 1,200 chariots and 60,000 horsemen in the fifth year of Rehoboam. He took the treasures of the temple and the royal palace.

name: Abijah vs Jeroboam
description: Abijah of Judah with 400,000 men faced Jeroboam of Israel with 800,000 on Mount Zemaraim. Despite being outnumbered, Judah cried to God and killed 500,000 of Israel's men.

name: Asa vs Zerah the Ethiopian
description: Zerah the Ethiopian came against Judah with a million men and 300 chariots. Asa cried to God: 'It is nothing for You to help.' God struck down the Ethiopians.

name: Battle of Ramoth-gilead (Ahab's Death)
description: Ahab allied with Jehoshaphat to retake Ramoth-gilead from Aram. Micaiah prophesied defeat. Ahab disguised himself but was struck by a random arrow and died.

name: Jehoshaphat's Victory by Praise
description: Moab, Ammon, and Mount Seir invaded Judah. Jehoshaphat prayed and God said 'the battle is not yours, but God's.' Judah sent singers ahead of the army. The enemies turned on each other.

name: Elisha and the Blinded Aramean Army
description: The king of Aram sent horses, chariots, and a great army to capture Elisha at Dothan. God struck them with blindness. Elisha led them to Samaria, where their sight was restored. Instead of killing them, Israel fed them and sent them home.
result: Aramean army captured and released; raids stopped

name: Aramean Siege of Samaria
description: Ben-hadad besieged Samaria until famine drove people to cannibalism. God caused the Arameans to hear the sound of a great army. They fled, leaving their camp full of provisions.

name: Jehu's Coup and Purge
description: Anointed by Elisha's servant, Jehu drove furiously to Jezreel. He killed King Joram of Israel with an arrow, had Jezebel thrown from a window, and exterminated the house of Ahab and all Baal worshipers.
result: Jehu seized the throne; Baal worship purged

name: Fall of Samaria (722 BC)
description: After a three-year siege, Shalmaneser V of Assyria captured Samaria. The northern kingdom of Israel was deported and scattered among the nations, ending the ten tribes as a sovereign entity.

name: Sennacherib's Siege of Jerusalem (701 BC)
description: Sennacherib of Assyria besieged Jerusalem after conquering 46 Judean cities. Hezekiah prayed. The angel of the LORD struck down 185,000 Assyrians in one night.

name: Josiah at Megiddo
description: Good King Josiah inexplicably tried to stop Pharaoh Necho from passing through to aid Assyria. Necho warned him, but Josiah disguised himself and was killed by archers at Megiddo.

name: Battle of Carchemish
description: Nebuchadnezzar defeated Pharaoh Necho, establishing Babylonian dominance. This led to Judah's captivity as prophesied by Jeremiah.

name: First Siege of Jerusalem (597 BC)
description: Nebuchadnezzar besieged Jerusalem. King Jehoiachin surrendered. Babylon took the king, his court, 10,000 captives (including Ezekiel), and the temple treasures.

name: Fall of Jerusalem (586 BC)
description: Nebuchadnezzar destroyed Solomon's Temple and Jerusalem. Judah was taken into Babylonian exile as God's judgment for covenant unfaithfulness.

name: Battle of Beth Horon (Maccabees)
description: Judas Maccabeus ambushed the Seleucid general Seron at the pass of Beth Horon, routing a much larger force with a small band of faithful fighters.

name: Battle of Emmaus
description: Judas Maccabeus with 3,000 poorly armed men defeated Gorgias's 5,000 infantry and 1,000 cavalry by attacking the Seleucid camp while Gorgias searched the hills for him.

name: Battle of Beth Zur
description: Judas Maccabeus defeated the Seleucid general Lysias at Beth Zur, opening the road to Jerusalem for the rededication of the Temple.

name: Rededication of the Temple (Hanukkah)
description: After recapturing Jerusalem, Judas Maccabeus cleansed and rededicated the Temple that Antiochus Epiphanes had defiled. The celebration lasted eight days — the origin of Hanukkah.

name: Massacre of the Innocents
description: Herod the Great, seeking to destroy the newborn King of the Jews, ordered the slaughter of all male children in Bethlehem two years old and under. Joseph fled with Mary and Jesus to Egypt.
result: Innocents killed; Jesus escaped to Egypt

name: War in Heaven (Michael vs Satan)
description: Michael and his angels fought against the dragon. The dragon and his angels were cast out of heaven to the earth. The great dragon, that ancient serpent called the devil and Satan, was thrown down.

name: Battle of Armageddon
description: The kings of the earth gathered at Armageddon (Har Megiddo) for the final battle. Christ returned with the armies of heaven. The beast and false prophet were thrown into the lake of fire.
belligerents: Christ and the armies of heaven
Beast, False Prophet, and kings of the earth
